# subagent-auto-manager LLM Guide

This package is a Codex hook plus CLI utility backed by SQLite. Use it only as a small supplement to Codex built-in agent tools.

Use it to check counts, states, and agent ids. Do not use it to replace `spawn_agent`, `wait_agent`, `resume_agent`, `close_agent`, or result gathering.

Minimize tool calls. Keep output small. Prefer counts first, then narrow id lists. Avoid broad/history/detail queries unless a human is debugging.

Do not include human-only debug commands in this file.

## Normal Workflow

```sh
# 1. Quick counts only. No runs.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest

# 2. Launch agents with Codex built-in spawn_agent.

# 3. Wait for every current running, not-closed agent.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest wait --timeout-ms 600000 --text
# During wait, newly stopped agent ids stream on stderr.

# 4. Get stopped agent ids only.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest --status stopped

# Optional: narrow running output to one known agent id.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest --agent <agent-id>

# 5. Gather each stopped agent with Codex built-in tools, then close_agent when done.

# 6. After gather/close workflow, bulk-hide completed stopped agents.
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest reset
```

Default no-arg output is summary JSON only, with no `runs` property:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "running": 12,
    "stopped": 0,
    "closed": 0,
    "total": 12,
    "shown": 12
  }
}
```

Filtered output defaults to compact run rows:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "running": 0,
    "stopped": 12,
    "closed": 0,
    "total": 12,
    "shown": 12
  },
  "runs": [
    { "agentId": "agent-a", "state": "stopped", "stopReason": "hook" },
    { "agentId": "agent-b", "state": "stopped", "stopReason": "pid-change" }
  ]
}
```

## Hook Logic

Hook command:

```sh
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest hook
```

Use this command for `SubagentStart`, `SubagentStop`, and `PostToolUse` close/resume hooks. After changing hooks, open `/hooks` in Codex and trust them.

PostToolUse matcher:

```text
(close_agent|resume_agent)$
```

The hook reads JSON from stdin, validates `hook_event_name`, requires `session_id`, resolves the project root from payload `cwd` or current working directory, records process identity, writes the ledger update, then prints `{}` to stdout.

Supported hook events:

```text
SubagentStart
SubagentStop
PostToolUse
```

Unsupported events fail the hook command. Unrelated `PostToolUse` payloads are ignored and are not written to the events table.

## Storage

Database path:

```text
<project>/.codex/subagent_auto_manager.db/ledger.sqlite3
```

SQLite uses WAL mode and two main tables:

```text
subagent_events: append-only rows for recorded supported hook events
subagent_runs: current derived row per run_key
```

The ledger stores compact raw JSON payloads plus extracted recall fields: ids, type, model, cwd, transcript paths, prompt, last assistant message, permission mode, turn id, tool name, tool call id, close target, Codex session PID, and legacy PID columns.

`SubagentStart` also stores `startArgs`, which is the start payload minus `hook_event_name`, `session_id`, `turn_id`, `transcript_path`, and `agent_transcript_path`.

## Run Identity

`run_key` selection:

```text
close/resume PostToolUse target -> <session_id>:<target>
known agent id                 -> <session_id>:<agent_id>
SubagentStop without agent id  -> latest running row in the session matching transcript_path, if any
fallback                       -> <session_id>:unknown:<16-char sha256 digest>
```

Known agent id fields are checked in this order:

```text
agent_id
subagent_id
agentId
subagentId
run_id
runId
```

`agent_type` / `agentType`, `turn_id` / `turnId`, `permission_mode` / `permissionMode`, transcript path variants, and last assistant message variants are also normalized.

## State Model

The internal run state is `status` plus `closed`:

```text
status: running | stopped
closed: boolean
public state: closed if closed, otherwise status
```

Meanings:

```text
running: SubagentStart seen; no SubagentStop; not closed.
stopped: SubagentStop seen, or marked stale after Codex session process change; not closed.
closed: public closed mark is set. close_agent means the parent thread closed; reset only bulk-hides rows after gather/close. resume_agent clears the target's closed mark.
```

`SubagentStop` means subagent execution ended. Real closed thread state is separate and comes from `PostToolUse` for `close_agent` / `resume_agent`; reset is a local ledger cleanup mark.

`stopReason` is derived:

```text
hook: stopped with a real SubagentStop event id.
pid-change: stopped by stale-run detection after a later SubagentStart came from a different Codex session process.
null: not stopped, or stopped without enough stop evidence.
```

Codex built-in multi-target `wait_agent` may return when any target completes. This package's `wait` is the wait-all helper and should keep returning success only after every requested target is `stopped`.

## Close And Resume Tool Logic

Only successful close/resume `PostToolUse` events affect `closed`.

Accepted close/resume tool names include:

```text
close_agent
resume_agent
multi_agent_v1close_agent
multi_agent_v1resume_agent
multi_agent_v1__close_agent
multi_agent_v1__resume_agent
multi_agent_v1.close_agent
multi_agent_v1.resume_agent
```

Do not generalize to `multi_agent_v2...` or `multi_agent_v10...`.

Close target lookup:

```text
tool_input.target, then payload target
```

Resume target lookup:

```text
tool_input.id, then payload id
```

Tool response may be an object or a JSON string. The response must not have `isError` / `is_error` set. Close requires `previous_status`; resume requires `status`. The required status field may be direct or under `structuredContent` / `structured_content`. A `not_found` status is ignored.

If close targets an unknown run, the ledger creates a stopped, closed placeholder row keyed by the target.

## Stale-Run Detection

On each `SubagentStart`, and before CLI `running`, `list`, `wait`, or `debug` reads the ledger, if the command can identify the current Codex session PID, any still-running rows in the same session with a different non-null Codex session PID are marked stopped. Their `stop_event_id` and `stop_payload` stay null, so public output shows `stopReason: "pid-change"`.

The hook records one meaningful PID:

```text
codexSessionPid: CODEX_PID if set, otherwise the first Codex ancestor process PID
```

Legacy `hook_parent_pid` and `hook_session_pid` fields are kept for schema/output compatibility and represent the same Codex session PID for new records and public output. A changed wrapper parent PID does not stop runs when the Codex session PID is unchanged.

## CLI Logic

Commands:

```text
running: default command, list current running agents or summary
list: list all statuses, intended for manual debugging
reset: hide completed stopped agents by marking them closed
wait: poll until all targets are stopped
hook: process one hook stdin payload
help / --help / -h
--version / -v
```

Defaults:

```text
session: --session, otherwise CODEX_THREAD_ID
cwd: --cwd, otherwise current working directory
output: JSON
status filter: running
wait timeout: 30000 ms
wait poll interval: 1000 ms
```

Output formats:

```text
--json
--yaml / --yml
--text
```

Detail levels:

```text
summary: summary only; no runs property.
compact: agentId, state, and stopReason when stopped.
medium: compact plus type, prompt, times, duration, last assistant message, startArgs, model, cwd.
full: all public run fields, parsed raw payloads, and full summary with sessionId.
```

Effective detail:

```text
no list/filter args -> summary
list/filter args    -> compact
explicit --medium   -> medium
explicit --full     -> full
```

Normal listing can filter by:

```text
--running
--stopped
--status running
--status stopped
--agent <id>
```

`--agent` matches `agentId`, `subagentId`, `runKey`, or `<session_id>:<agent_id>`.

Broad all-status listings, closed-only listings, full history windows, full reset, and clearing one closed mark are human-debug operations. Do not use them during normal agent work and do not add their commands to this file.

The CLI writes a one-line diagnostic hint to stderr after list/running output. The primary machine-readable result stays on stdout.

## Wait Logic

Wait commands:

```sh
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest wait --timeout-ms 600000 --text
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest wait --agent <agent-a> --agent <agent-b> --timeout-ms 600000 --text
```

Targets come from positional ids and `--agent` values. With no explicit targets, `wait` snapshots the current running, not-closed rows at the start. With `--all`, it adds the current running, not-closed rows to any explicit targets.

A wait target matches:

```text
agentId
subagentId
runKey
<session_id>:<target>
```

Target states:

```text
stopped: matched run has internal status stopped.
running: matched run has internal status running.
missing: no matching ledger row in this session/project ledger.
```

Wait completes successfully only when every target is `stopped` and none are `missing`. On timeout, exit code is 1. `running` and `missing` targets are incomplete.

During polling, each newly stopped target is emitted once to stderr:

```text
[subagent-auto-manager] wait stopped agentId=<id> type=<agent_type>
```

On timeout, stderr emits one timeout line per incomplete target. Final JSON/YAML/text output stays on stdout.

Text output labels:

```text
Pending: matched run is still running.
Stopped: matched run is stopped.
Miss: target did not match any run.
Closed: list output label for public closed state; closed alone does not make wait complete.
```

On successful text wait, all targets are listed. On timeout text wait, only incomplete targets are listed on stdout.

## Reset Logic

Default `reset` marks stopped, not-closed rows in the current session as closed:

```sh
npx -y subagent-auto-manager@latest reset
```

This is intended after the parent gathered stopped agents and closed their threads with Codex built-in tools.

Other reset modes are human-debug operations and are intentionally omitted here.

## Output Shape Notes

Summary counts always come from all rows in the selected session:

```text
running: status running and not closed
stopped: status stopped and not closed
closed: closed rows, regardless of internal status
total: all rows in the session
shown: rows returned by the current filter
```

Run ordering is:

```text
open rows before closed rows
running before stopped
newest start_time first
```

Text list output starts with:

```text
session <session_id> total=<n> running=<n> stopped=<n> closed=<n>
```

Then each run uses:

```text
Pending <agentId> <agentType> <elapsed> <prompt>
Stopped <agentId> <agentType> <duration> stop=<reason>
Closed <agentId> <agentType> <duration> stop=<reason>
```

Prompts are collapsed to one line and truncated in text output.

## Avoid

Do not ask for all states, closed-only lists, historical lists, full payloads, or human-debug reset modes during normal agent work. These create large output and are for human debugging only.
